text:
Market today: First equity outflow since March signals the easy-money momentum trade is stalling. Risk-off rotation hits high-beta tech and chips hardest — exactly your book. Margin amplifies every down tick.

Your holdings: PLTR is the most urgent — down 17.6% and flagged SELL; you're bleeding on margin and the stop at $85 is still well below current price, so size matters here. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.2% and flagged TRIM — a big name under pressure with the most dollar exposure. SPAL is a 2x leveraged SpaceX ETF, down 16.5% — decay and leverage compound losses fast; this one can go to zero if you sit on it. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; margin on this is playing with fire.

Watch: (1) Whether outflow trend continues — a second week confirms risk-off is real, not a blip. (2) Micron analyst chatter — if MU moves, SMH and AVGO follow.

Bottom line: Cut or size down PLTR and AVGO now — margin means you don't get the luxury of waiting for a bounce.
